symbols.name: repo_manual
symbols.docstring: repo-manual: an AI-authored, human-read orientation manual that lives in the repo.

symbols.name: cli
symbols.docstring: The ``repo-manual`` CLI — the deterministic harness; the narrative is written by the *orchestrator*

symbols.name: init
symbols.signature: (path: str=typer.Argument('.', help='Repo root to initialize.')) -> None
symbols.docstring: Create the ``.repo-manual/`` folder and config (idempotent).

symbols.name: scan
symbols.signature: (path: str=typer.Argument('.', help='Repo root to scan.')) -> None
symbols.docstring: Analyze the repo and write only the deterministic structural index (no pages).

symbols.name: generate
symbols.signature: (path: str=typer.Argument('.', help='Repo root.'), provider: str=typer.Option('none', help="LLM orchestrator. 'none' = deterministic skeletons.")) -> None
symbols.docstring: Scan + plan + write the manual. ``--provider none`` writes skeletons for an orchestrator to fill.

symbols.name: plan
symbols.signature: (path: str=typer.Argument('.', help='Repo root.'), as_json: bool=typer.Option(False, '--json', help='Print the raw plan.json.')) -> None
symbols.docstring: Show the pending generation tasks — what the orchestrator should narrate next, and from where.

symbols.name: stale
symbols.signature: (path: str=typer.Argument('.', help='Repo root.'), check: bool=typer.Option(False, '--check', help='Exit non-zero if any page has drifted (for a pre-commit hook).'), include_pending: bool=typer.Option(False, '--include-pending', help='With --check, also fail on never-narrated pages.')) -> None
symbols.docstring: List pages that are out of date (drifted source) or never narrated.

symbols.name: ingest
symbols.docstring: Promote orchestrator-filled skeletons to narrated pages and pin their freshness hashes.

symbols.name: structure
symbols.docstring: Emit the grouping brief + a suggested ``structure.json`` for the orchestrator to turn package

symbols.name: verify
symbols.signature: (path: str=typer.Argument('.', help='Repo root.'), strict: bool=typer.Option(False, '--strict', help='Also fail when a narrated page cites <2 sources.')) -> None
symbols.docstring: Check every narrated page's source citations resolve to real files and line ranges (no LLM).

symbols.name: brief
symbols.signature: (page_id: str=typer.Argument(..., help='The page id to narrate (see `repo-manual plan`).'), path: str=typer.Option('.', '--path', help='Repo root.'), as_json: bool=typer.Option(False, '--json', help='Emit the raw GenerationTask JSON.')) -> None
symbols.docstring: Print one page's complete narration brief — the files to ground in, the symbol outline, and the

symbols.name: hook
symbols.signature: (path: str=typer.Argument('.', help='Repo root.'), install: bool=typer.Option(False, '--install', help='Write the snippet to .git/hooks/pre-commit.')) -> None
symbols.docstring: Print (or --install) a git pre-commit hook that flags a manual gone stale + broken citations.

symbols.name: config
symbols.docstring: ``ManualConfig`` — the small, committed ``.repo-manual/manual.config.json`` that pins where the

symbols.name: ManualConfig
symbols.docstring: Resolved config. ``root`` is the absolute repo root; the rest is what lands in the JSON file.

symbols.name: load
symbols.docstring: Load ``<root>/<output>/manual.config.json`` if present, else return defaults.

symbols.name: freshness
symbols.docstring: Freshness — the wedge. Each narrated page pins the content-hash of every source file it was

symbols.name: current_hash
symbols.docstring: Hash a source file as it exists on disk now, or ``None`` if it no longer exists.

symbols.name: page_status
symbols.docstring: Re-evaluate one page's freshness against the working tree (does not mutate the page).

symbols.name: refresh
symbols.docstring: Recompute and write back every page's ``status``. Returns the {page_id: status} map.

symbols.name: stale_pages
symbols.docstring: Pages an orchestrator still owes work on: never narrated (PENDING) or drifted (STALE).

symbols.name: hashing
symbols.docstring: Content hashing for freshness. One short, stable helper used by the analyzer (to stamp each

symbols.name: model
symbols.docstring: The in-memory IR: a deterministic **structural index** of a repo (`RepoIndex`) and the

symbols.name: _enum_or
symbols.docstring: Parse an enum value, falling back to ``default`` for unknown/missing values instead of raising —

symbols.name: SourceFile
symbols.docstring: One analyzed source file. ``path`` is repo-relative & POSIX; ``content_hash`` powers freshness.

symbols.name: Symbol
symbols.docstring: A definition. ``id`` is ``<file>::<qualname>`` (module symbol: ``<file>::<module>``).

symbols.name: Edge
symbols.docstring: A directed relationship. For ``IMPORTS`` src/dst are file paths; for ``CALLS`` they are

symbols.name: RepoIndex
symbols.docstring: The whole deterministic picture: files, the symbols defined in them, and the edges between.

symbols.name: PageSource
symbols.docstring: Provenance of a page's body. ``SKELETON`` = deterministic stub awaiting an orchestrator;

symbols.name: FileRef
symbols.docstring: A page's link to a source file, pinned to the hash it was last narrated against (freshness).

symbols.name: Page
symbols.docstring: One chapter of the manual: a system/subsystem the human reads. The deterministic core sets

symbols.name: Section
symbols.docstring: A grouping of pages. ``slug`` is the on-disk folder under ``manual/``.

symbols.name: Manual
symbols.docstring: The structure index: ordered sections + the flat page map. Persisted to ``manual.json``.

symbols.name: GenerationTask
symbols.docstring: Everything the orchestrator needs to narrate one page, with no hidden state. Emitted by the

symbols.name: plan
symbols.docstring: The structure planner — how the repo is carved into the pages a human reads.

symbols.name: plan_manual
symbols.docstring: Build the deterministic manual structure (sections + pages) from a `RepoIndex`.

symbols.name: System
symbols.docstring: One orchestrator-decided system = one page that may span several files.

symbols.name: plan_from_structure
symbols.docstring: Build the manual from authored systems (one page per system). Returns (manual, warnings).

symbols.name: structure_brief
symbols.docstring: The grounding an orchestrator needs to DECIDE systems: every file with its module docstring,

symbols.name: suggested_structure
symbols.docstring: A deterministic starting point for structure.json (package-based), for the orchestrator to edit

symbols.name: task_for
symbols.signature: (index: RepoIndex, manual: Manual, page: Page) -> GenerationTask
symbols.docstring: Assemble the orchestrator brief for one page from the index (symbols + internal deps).

symbols.name: _importance
symbols.signature: (path: str, module: str, in_degree: int) -> Importance

symbols.name: _overview_page
symbols.docstring: The 'start here' page. Grounded in the repo's entry points so the narrative can open with how

symbols.name: scan
symbols.docstring: Language analyzers: turn a repo's source into a `RepoIndex`. Python ships first; the

symbols.name: base
symbols.docstring: The pluggable analyzer seam. A `LanguageAnalyzer` walks a configured repo and returns a

symbols.name: LanguageAnalyzer
symbols.docstring: Static analysis for one language. Implementations must be pure & deterministic: same bytes in,

symbols.name: iter_source_files
symbols.docstring: Walk ``config.source_dirs`` (or the repo root) yielding files with a matching suffix, honoring

symbols.name: get_analyzer
symbols.docstring: Return the analyzer for ``language`` (currently just Python). Raises on unknown languages.

symbols.name: python
symbols.docstring: The Python analyzer: parse every ``.py`` file with the stdlib ``ast`` and emit a deterministic

symbols.name: _ModuleSymbols
symbols.docstring: Top-level name -> symbol id, used to resolve calls and `from x import name` bindings.

symbols.name: _module_name
symbols.docstring: ``src/pkg/mod.py`` (source_dir ``src``) -> ``pkg.mod``; ``__init__.py`` -> the package.

symbols.name: _def_symbol
symbols.signature: (self, p: _Parsed, node: ast.AST, qualname: str, kind: SymbolKind) -> Symbol

symbols.name: _resolve_from_module
symbols.signature: (self, current_module: str, node: ast.ImportFrom) -> str | None

symbols.name: _nearest_internal_file
symbols.signature: (self, module: str, module_to_file: dict[str, str]) -> str | None
symbols.docstring: Map a dotted module to an indexed file, walking up parents (``a.b.c`` -> ``a.b`` -> ``a``).

symbols.name: _call_edges
symbols.signature: (self, p: _Parsed, module_to_file: dict[str, str], top_tables: dict[str, _ModuleSymbols]) -> list[Edge]

symbols.name: _callables
symbols.docstring: Yield ``(symbol_id, body_node)`` for each function/method whose calls we attribute.

symbols.name: _callee_name
symbols.docstring: The simple name being called: ``f()`` -> ``f``; ``obj.method()`` -> ``method``.

symbols.name: store
symbols.docstring: The ``.repo-manual/`` store: the folder IS the source of truth. This module writes (and safely

symbols.name: load_structure
symbols.docstring: Load the orchestrator-authored ``structure.json`` (the AI grouping), if present.

symbols.name: build_manual
symbols.docstring: Pick the grouping: authored systems (``structure.json``) if present, else the package seed.

symbols.name: write_structure_brief
symbols.docstring: Write the grouping brief + a deterministic suggested structure for the orchestrator to edit

symbols.name: init_store
symbols.docstring: Create ``.repo-manual/`` + config + a short README. Idempotent.

symbols.name: write_manual
symbols.signature: (config: ManualConfig, index: RepoIndex, manual: Manual) -> WriteResult
symbols.docstring: Write the whole store, preserving any existing narrative + human edits.

symbols.name: write_index
symbols.docstring: Write just the structural index (``index/*.json``) — used by ``scan`` without touching pages.

symbols.name: load_index
symbols.docstring: Load the committed structural index (``index/*.json``), or None if it hasn't been written yet.

symbols.name: _write_page
symbols.signature: (config: ManualConfig, index: RepoIndex, manual: Manual, page: Page, prev: Manual | None) -> bool
symbols.docstring: Write one page file. Returns True if an existing narrative was preserved (vs a fresh skeleton).

symbols.name: _render_skeleton
symbols.signature: (config: ManualConfig, index: RepoIndex, manual: Manual, page: Page) -> str

symbols.name: _carry_forward
symbols.docstring: Copy provenance from the previous manual onto freshly-planned pages so narrated pages keep

symbols.name: ingest_filled_pages
symbols.signature: (config: ManualConfig, manual: Manual, now: str) -> list[str]
symbols.docstring: Detect pages an orchestrator has narrated (pending marker gone from the .md generated region) and

symbols.name: _rewrite_frontmatter
symbols.docstring: Re-emit a page's frontmatter from its (now updated) metadata, preserving both fenced regions.

symbols.name: _write_plan
symbols.signature: (out: Path, index: RepoIndex, manual: Manual) -> None

symbols.name: _source_link
symbols.signature: (config: ManualConfig, page: Page, relpath: str) -> str

symbols.name: _relative_page_link
symbols.signature: (manual: Manual, page: Page, other_page_id: str) -> str
symbols.docstring: Link from this page's section folder to another page's file, resolving its section folder.

symbols.name: _extract_region
symbols.signature: (text: str, start: str, end: str) -> str | None

symbols.name: verify
symbols.docstring: Citation validation — the trust gate. The narrative's whole credibility rests on the recipe's rule:

symbols.name: verify_manual
symbols.signature: (config: ManualConfig, manual: Manual, strict: bool=False) -> VerifyReport

symbols.name: _line_count
symbols.signature: (root: Path, rel: str, cache: dict[str, int | None]) -> int | None
